programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio and the team discover a woman's body in a secret tunnel leading to a bank vault and begin checking into her background. DNA evidence leads them to a man employed at a nearby building site, but subsequent developments implicate a millionaire's wayward daughter - and a bogus art dealer with links to the drug trade. Starring David Caruso and Adam Rodriguez

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A jet skier with a shady past is killed with a piece of slate that later proves to have come from Alexx's garden, raising suspicions her son Bryan may be the killer. Determined to protect him, she launches a private investigation, following a trail of clues to an FBI agent with prior connections to the victim. Crime drama, starring Khandi Alexander in her final regular appearance on the show

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A bungled Swat raid on a residence results in the death of an innocent man, and separate chains of evidence lead Horatio to two equally viable suspects - the victim's business partner, and the man with whom his wife was having an affair. Tom Sizemore guest stars as a dubious private eye linked to the case

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The CSIs uncover a plot to distribute a new type of ammunition designed to explode on impact, and the ensuing investigation brings Horatio into contact with old adversary Ron Saris - a reunion destined to have deadly consequences. Drama, starring David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez

programme.desc: A patient's deathbed murder confession presents Dr Finlay with a crisis of conscience, over whether to keep a secret entrusted to him in strictest confidence or see to it that justice is done

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew encounters a patient whose sister is unwittingly killing him with kindness, while Sam encourages a grieving husband to pick up the pieces of his shattered life and accept responsibility for his baby daughter _ in spite of her heartbreaking legacy. On the personal front, Joanna confides in Carol about her one-night stand at the college reunion. Rural drama, starring Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan has a mishap while treating a cow, leaving the Darrowby practice facing a possible lawsuit. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William is headhunted by a French accountancy firm, and he and Hester decide to move to France. However, the pair soon discover that what divides the British and French is more than just the English Channel. Sequel to Fresh Fields, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom grows tired of watching Geoff fill every available space with shelves, and Diana feels restricted in her new role as nanny and decides to return to Bayview. Meanwhile, a postcard from Marion makes Geoff realise how much he misses her. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen prepare to meet their maker as they face a firing squad, while Madame Fanny discovers Edith in bed with the Fuehrer

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley finally finishes his off-the-road motorised scooter, while bored son-in-law Barry longs for a walk on the wild side. Rural comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Thora Hird

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth uncovers a dark secret when a contestant is murdered at a ballroom dancing competition. Drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of pornographer Jim Hockney is reopened when his daughter Sara provides DNA proof he wasn't her father. The team tries to track down jazz singer Angela Gold, who was the victim's girlfriend at the time and disappeared from the music scene after being acquitted of the crime, and it's Brian's replacement Danny Griffin (Nicholas Lyndhurst) who manages to find her. However, with Angela professing her innocence and Sara's mother Jane also arousing suspicion, it becomes a struggle to determine who is telling the truth. With a guest appearance by former Corrie killer Brian Capron

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: MI5 discovers a notorious Islamic terrorist is planning to attack the UK, so Adam and Zafar travel to Turkey in a bid to persuade him to join them instead. Despite the apparent success of the mission, the team is uncertain whether the new recruit means exactly what he says. Drama, starring Rupert Penry-Jones and Raza Jaffrey

programme.title: Gregory's Girl (1981)
programme.desc: A gawky teenager is dismayed to be relegated to goalkeeper in the school football team - but his thoughts turn to romance once he meets the female striker who takes his place. However, his awkward nature proves an obstacle to winning her over, despite the sage advice of his worldly-wise little sister. Comedy, starring John Gordon Sinclair, Dee Hepburn, Jake D'Arcy, Clare Grogan and Robert Buchanan

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: President Lincoln's visit sets in motion a diabolical rebel plot and offers and opportunity to free James Green. Meanwhile, Dr Foster uncovers a scheme to undermine him at the expense of his patients and Aurelia recovers from her surgery and makes a bold decision

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine

programme.desc: Henry and Jess' meal together proves productive, Simon and Anna go out on a date and Karen worries about an elderly patient's grand-daughter. Drama about community nurses living and working in a small Yorkshire town, starring Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash

Meanwhile, the friendship between Margaret and Bob Bishop arouses suspicion. Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed star

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Maverick British rifleman Richard Sharpe is promoted for saving General Wellesley's life, and sent behind enemy lines to rally Spanish support. Surrounded by the French, he manages to pull off an inspiring propaganda coup in the small town of Torrecastro. Swashbuckling adventure, featuring Downton Abbey creator Julian Fellowes and starring Sean Bean and Brian Cox

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Sequel to hit fantasy drama Life on Mars. Alex Drake, a single mother working as a police psychologist in 2008 is shocked to wake up in London in 1981 after being shot. DCI Gene Hunt, DS Ray Carling and DC Chris Skelton, transferred to the Met, swiftly come to her rescue. She knows all about the trio having spent months reading Sam Tyler's reports of his time in the past, but cannot understand why she is sharing his delusion. Starring Philip Glenister and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: DCI Hunt is determined nothing will spoil Diana Spencer's wedding, and so sets out to prevent a potentially explosive protest getting out of hand. When it seems Thatcherite playboy Danny is in danger, Alex is assigned to look after him - but after a speedy ride in a DeLorean leaves her in no fit state for work, Gene and Ray set out to crack the case. Philip Glenister and Keeley Hawes star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently hunt for the murderer of a woman whose body is discovered in a local church. As the investigation progresses, it turns out the victim was a waitress working at the first hostess club in Newcastle - an establishment that has greatly upset the local religious community. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with guest appearances by Brendan Coyle (Lark Rise to Candleford) and Mark Williams (The Fast Show)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When an animal activist is killed on the first day of duck-hunting season, Mike and the team must determine whether it was an accidental shooting, or something more sinister

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne is duped into finding an Australian Rules football coach's missing lucky cap, and instead discovers the body of a local team captain hanging by a rival team's scarf. The subsequent investigation is complicated by the return of her fiance and Jack's ex-wife. Crime drama, starring Essie Davis

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A head teacher confronts the dealers preying on his community as the team deal with the consequences of a surge in drug-related crime. With Colin Salmon

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: First episode of the Glasgow-set crime drama. A murderer is on the loose, and one by one, bodies of young women are found strangled in the city. The inquiry is headed by top detective Jim Taggart, a tough local cop who has risen through the ranks and one of the new breed of graduate policemen, Peter Livingstone. However, despite their differences they work together breaking all the rules to hunt the killer before he strikes again. Mark McManus and Neil Duncan star

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: The staff unite to save one of their own. A former slave turned activist arrives in town, causing a rift between Foster and Mary. Emma and Frank's romance ignites and Samuel plans for a reunion with Aurelia. Ridley Scott's historical drama, starring Mary Elizabeth Winstead, Josh Radnor and Gary Cole

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: A house guest becomes the focus of Alice's schemes. When Mary falls gravely ill, Foster's attempts to care for her antagonise the new chief. Charlotte tries to contain the smallpox epidemic and Samuel has to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey's partying catches up with him when he wakes up in a strange bed and ends up being grilled at Mill Street station on rape charges. Will takes a blood sample from an unconscious driver who has knocked a courier off his bike, and takes advice from Matt about whether it is admissible evidence in court. When the sample goes missing, he jumps to conclusions about his love-rival's determination to make life difficult for him

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Honey is horrified to learn Matt has taken over all the wedding arrangements and warns Emma not to rush blindly into marriage. A prostitute found beaten unconscious turns out to be a drugs mule working for Paul Haskew and Jack finally realises how deeply Zain is involved in the criminal activities of Kristen Shaw. Sam angles for a date with Phil and Stuart is told to mind his own business

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates the disappearance of a prostitute whose evidence helped put away rapist Martin Delaney. Zain finally persuades Kristen Shaw to name her supplier and puts himself in great danger to arrange a meeting with the mysterious Jose Alvarez. Phil apologises to Sam for missing their date and agrees to take things slowly, but Stuart makes it clear he's not going to be pushed out without a fight

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A young tearaway is brought in for questioning about a break-in at a doctor's surgery, but it soon becomes clear a staff member has something to hide. Yvonne loses confidence about her chances of landing the sergeant's job at Sun Hill and decides to look elsewhere for promotion. Emma chooses the wrong moment to tell Matt about her night of passion with Will

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Dr Harrison tells Rev Hutton of his intentions toward Sophy and asks permission to court her, blissfully unaware that the generosity he has shown to Mrs Rose has accidentally created an unlikely love triangle. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The true nature of friendship within Cranford is revealed as the ladies rally to help Miss Matty when she receives terrible news. Meanwhile, Dr Harrison suffers as his career, reputation and relationship all seem ruined, while Miss Galindo and Mr Carter grow closer as they face up to the tragedy of Hanbury. Judi Dench, Philip Glenister and Simon Woods star

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: First in a two-part special. The story picks up in August 1844, two years after Miss Matty lost her beloved sister Deborah. Mr Buxton has returned to live quietly in Cranford following the death of his wife, although Miss Matty is concerned about his relationship with his son as neither seem to be coping with their bereavement. The local busybody is also worried about Peggy Bell, who lives in an isolated cottage looking after her inattentive mother and domineering brother, so she hatches a plot to bring these young residents together. However, the surprise arrival of Lady Ludlow's son Lord Septimus leads to a chain of unexpected events concerning the looming railway, and a tragedy at Matty's own house forces her to re-evaluate her opinions on the subject. Judi Dench and Jonathan Pryce star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Fifteen Streets
programme.desc: Feature-length drama based on Catherine Cookson's novel set in early 20th-century Newcastle. Worker John O'Brien lives near poverty in a small house in the workers district, yet falls in love with teacher Mary, daughter of a rich family. Despite the pressures exerted by the social difference, the vigorous Mary refuses to allow outer circumstances destroy their relationship. Starring Sean Bean and Clare Holman

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Tony worries Bren will be distracted during her TV debut. Stan hopes for a proposal and Dolly is so upset at the design of the new uniforms that she considers drastic action. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the sitcom, featuring interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show on its 20th anniversary

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty and amiable sidekick Geoffrey become embroiled in a convoluted case involving a deaf birdwatcher who goes missing at the same time as a woman's body is found at the bottom of a cliff he is known to frequent. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge and Dominic Monaghan, featuring a guest appearance by David Bower

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nurses and midwives of Nonnatus House prepare to celebrate Sister Monica Joan's birthday, but plans are put on hold when the closure of a nearby maternity home leaves them inundated with new patients, many of whom are in urgent need of support. Lucille treats a woman whose father is offering shelter to an old friend suffering with memory loss, and as November 22, 1963 arrives, the residents of Poplar are rocked by shocking news from America

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: In the tense build-up to a world trade summit in Edinburgh, Rebus looks forward to a well-earned day off - until Siobhan rings to say a body has been found in the grounds of the hotel hosting the conference. When a government minister is later killed in a fall from the castle ramparts, the abrasive copper comes up against Commander Steelforth of the SO12 unit providing security for the summit and, not for the first time, finds himself at odds with the Establishment. Julie Graham guest stars, with Ken Stott and Claire Price

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Cassie Booth goes missing while doing her local paper round, and her blood-stained clothes are later found in a nearby boathouse owned by Karl Wilding. He is the prime suspect, but then seemingly helpful Stephen Warrington, a wine merchant who has initially brought a complaint about a house being used as a brothel, demonstrates too much interest in the case

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A doctor is accused of murdering a seriously ill patient with a morphine overdose, a case which echoes a dilemma in Deed's personal life and sets him on the path to potential ruin. Meanwhile, Charlie's HIV-positive friend finds herself in danger of losing her baby to Social Services, and calls on the judge for help. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William are disappointed to learn Emma and Peter have made arrangements to be elsewhere over the festive period, so decide to treat their new French friends and neighbours to a typically English Christmas. But as the coronavirus hits, everybody in the housing market is affected

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets the residents of town in Kent that's slowly sliding into the sea, to see how they are coping with their perilous situation. Plus, the automatic bill switching services that have left some customers wondering whether they'd have been better off doing it themselves

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three flats in a terrible state of repair in a corner property in Wolverhampton and two terraces in Mountain Ash in Wales and Chatham in Kent become available on the market. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell take a look and follow progress as one seasoned developer and two novices tackle their projects

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the Mackintosh at the Willow tearooms in Glasgow to celebrate all things 20th century and collectable. Plus, expert Ben Cooper gives the lowdown on his family connection with Whitefriars glass and Tim Weeks shows off some collectable tech

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem and Kamini uncover the real plan behind New Trefelin, and it begins to look as if foul play may be the cause of the mine's collapse. If it's no accident, Aled and Daf are poised to become the prime suspects - but they protest their innocence. Meanwhile, Robert pleads with Megan to leave the village with him, but Prem confronts him with the truth about his past and Basil's real intentions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: In the final instalment of their culinary journey through the history of British cuisine, Si King and Dave Myers explore comfort food, discussing how certain ingredients and recipes can give people a sense of well-being, such as chicken noodle soup and a simple fish dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Carl Hutchinson present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo, actor Tom Davis, actor and musician Shaun Ryder and comedian Henning Wehn, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is furious with Lola for telling Denise he has been seeing Raymond. Peter gives Kathy's dating app a little tweak in the hope she will have more luck. Sharon worries that Bobby's fears may be getting out of control, while Kush is forced to pay up in exchange for his silence

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts the satirical quiz, inviting journalist Helen Lewis and comedian, actress and writer Janey Godley to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Legendary football manager Arsene Wenger talks about his autobiography My Life in Red and White, while Hollywood star Samuel L Jackson - joined by his wife LaTanya Richardson Jackson - discusses presenting the documentary Enslaved: The Lost History of the Transatlantic Slave Trade. Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora impulsively dyes her hair after a fight with her father, and Wally goes to a single-parent support group meeting. Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Nora suffers a vaginal injury, she's tapped by a DJ to record her chronic queefing for his new track. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Donna Hay, and special guest Sara Cox. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: This time, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for low cost baking, using ingredients that do not break the bank or compromise on flavour. Dishes include fluffy onion pretzels, a potato rosti quiche, rhubarb and custard kisses, and her signature rich and indulgent chocolate fondant

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton and Manchester City v Arsenal

programme.title: Rob Burrow: My Year with MND
programme.desc: Rugby league star Rob Burrow talks about his fight against motor neurone disease, and how his rugby career has prepared him for the challenges he faces

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Leeds Rhinos v Salford Red Devils (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the final from Wembley Stadium. Rhinos were in good form as they defeated Wigan Warriors 26-12 in the semi-finals, while Salford produced a fine comeback to prevail 24-22 against holders Warrington Wolves. With analysis from Jon Wilkin, Jamie Peacock and Shaun Wane, and commentary by Dave Woods

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Another four famous faces try the famous black chair out for size. They are antiques expert Philip Serrell, poet and presenter Lemn Sissay, Sky Sports anchor Olivia Wayne, and comedian Richard McCourt - better known as one half of the comedy duo Dick and Dom. John Humphrys asks the questions

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous sitcom actors take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tim Healy, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Kerry Godliman, Tom Basden, Mina Anwar, Serena Evans, Judy Cornwell and Jeremy Gittins

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the launch of this year's Strictly Come Dancing, joined in the ballroom by 12 brand new celebrity contestants, and judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood and Shirley Ballas. As well as revealing this year's pairings, the show will feature two spectacular group routines from the Strictly professionals, a very special music performance from Joel Corry and MNEK, and the return of reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Lynne and Graham, a couple from Merthyr Tydfi in Wales, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park and Manchester City v Arsenal at Etihad Stadium. It was 10 years to the day that Everton last recorded a victory over their neighbours, but they had started the season well. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking to avenge Arsenal's 2-0 victory when the sides last met three months ago in an FA Cup semi-final. Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the game between Kansas City Chiefs and Buffalo Bills and look ahead to the other fixtures in week six

programme.title: Dream House (2011)
programme.desc: A man moves to a small town with his wife and two daughters, but receives the startling news that a woman and her children were murdered in his new home five years previously. He begins seeing things which make him suspect the house is haunted, and that there may be a connection between the ghosts and his own past. Horror, starring Daniel Craig, Naomi Watts and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sima Kotecha present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park and Manchester City v Arsenal at Etihad Stadium. It was 10 years to the day that Everton last recorded a victory over their neighbours, but they had started the season well. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking to avenge Arsenal's 2-0 victory when the sides last met three months ago in an FA Cup semi-final. Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and pays a visit to a local jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Cheddar Gorge in Somerset to hear the origins of the hymn Rock of Ages, while Gemma Hunt meets worship leader Martin Smith and new Christian group Bright City

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple returning from living in Sweden for 25 years who want to find a home in Wiltshire, and goes for a ride with a professional carriage driver

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Wales, upcycling expert EJ Osborne uses his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre near Abergavenny by searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A pair of bench ends are worked on by woodworking specialists Josh and Oli, while designer maker Rupert Blanchard is given the tall order of transforming a tallboy chest of drawers. For his own project, EJ has a collection of glass jars to keep him busy

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent events, from a possible Banksy to an African spear linked to the explorer David Livingstone. Other finds include a Dior necklace, a collection of 1960s airline cabin bags and a remarkable skeleton clock, as well as two items related to the Suffragettes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Celebrating his win in a newspaper libel case, cabinet minister Peter Laurence is summoned to Downing Street to see PM Dawn Ellison, who reveals she is looking to promote him to an office of state. However, he is soon bought back down to earth with a bump when his special adviser Duncan Knock reveals an inmate in a women's prison is claiming to have a secret about his past that could affect his future. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Crystal Palace v Brighton & Hove Albion at Selhurst Park and Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Palace and Brighton shared the spoils in a 1-1 draw here last season, and Spurs won 2-0 against their London rivals here four months ago. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester Untied

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob catches up with celebrated Turner Prize-winning artist Chris Ofili, as he completes work on The Caged Bird's Song, a new piece set to receive its exhibition debut at London's National Gallery. Best known as a painter, Ofili switches mediums with his latest piece, working alongside expert weavers in Edinburgh to create a wool tapestry seven meters wide and three meters tall. With The Caged Bird's Song, Ofili aims to demonstrate his ongoing fascination with classical mythology and contemporary 'demigods', while also paying tribute to the landscape of his adopted island home of Trinidad

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling a set of binoculars salvaged from a U-Boat, two boxing belts awarded to a celebrated Tongan heavyweight, a vintage mechanical money box and an old wooden high chair

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Warrington Wolves v Leeds Rhinos, Hull FC v Huddersfield Giants and St Helens v Wakefield Trinity

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of economic recipes, including a Bake Off masterclass featuring Sussex suet pond pudding, and a James Martin tomato soup with garlic bread

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, market trader Gary Barton and blacksmith upcycler Kevin Paxton head to St Andrews to find some bargains they can turn into profit for Children in Need

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George has a new noisy neighbour, and it is the occupant of the house up the road that he wanted to buy, and the seat of his childhood memories

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The seven home sewers take on man-made fabrics designed for sport and the great outdoors, while the transformation challenge involves discarded festival tents. For the final assignment, male models arrive in the Sewing Room to be fitted with made-to-measure luxury tracksuits. Joe Lycett presents, while Esme Young and Patrick Grant pass judgment

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan sets out to gain a new understanding of fierce predators by meeting different tribes that hunt and live alongside them. He begins on Owarigi in the Solomon Islands, where the local men encounter dangerous sharks almost every day, even swimming alongside them as they freedive for the fish they need to survive

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are at Glemham Hall in the Suffolk countryside, where David Fletcher spots a rare flintlock pistol, and James Lewis uncovers a copy of a Raphael painting

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Mark and Adam Robertson finally get their trawler Zephyr back in the water after a break for repairs, but just 30 miles out they suffer an engine problem and have to turn back to shore. On the Reliance, David Clark is four days through his first trip as skipper when he faces an inspection by Marine Scotland

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson demonstrates dishes that combine the comfort of the familiar with the excitement of the new, including fluffy oat pancakes drizzled with a raspberry and honey syrup. She offers a twist on a classic Caesar salad for a light lunch idea, and rustles up a Greek-inspired supper for friends including squid and orzo pasta, and a sweet and savoury version of a cheesecake. Nigella's simple rice bowl with ginger, radish and avocado celebrates a few choice ingredients and results in a dish that's uplifting to look at, as well as to eat

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedians Lolly Adefope and Ellie Taylor, musician Ricky Wilson and presenter and author Richard Osman, who reveal the books that make them cry and the fictional characters they most relate to. They discuss this week's book group picks - Love After Love by Ingrid Persaud and Richard's debut novel, The Thursday Murder Club

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie heads to a friend's farm where a young calf has developed a serious limp. The calf's protective mother is making it clear she wants her baby back, and Jennie does not want to distress her, so has to work fast. Vet Darren treats a two-day-old lamb, whose owner thinks has a urinary infection. However, when she happily relieves herself all over Darren's examination table, he has to use all his investigative skills to diagnose the real problem. Maurice King, the founder of the Downe Veterinary Clinic, encounters a loggerhead turtle has been rescued by the local sea life sanctuary

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnie's labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don prepares his pond for the season ahead and prunes a climbing rose. Adam Frost offers ideas for a winter display of containers and the programme catches up with Frances Tophill on her allotment in Kent, to find out about her growing year. Plus, another chance to see Carol Klein's autumnal visit to Hidcote Gardens in Gloucestershire, and a couple whose garden has been inspired by the Far East

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Paloma Faith chats with Jools ahead of the release of her fifth album Infinite Things, the follow-up to 2017's number one The Architect. Her forthcoming record was completed before the pandemic, but during lockdown she changed it dramatically to reflect the times. A selection of her earlier work features amid a diverse selection of clips from the show's archive. Plus, a performance at Jools' studio from producer, songwriter and musician Fraser T Smith. Famed for his work with the likes of Adele and rappers Stormzy and Dave, he's now stepping out with his own music. There's also a performance by Malian songwriter and guitarist Afel Bocoum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Roslin fears the worst when a convicted felon is nominated to become the next vice president, and her advisers tell her she must do everything in her power to prevent it from happening. Meanwhile, on Caprica, Helo gets a wake-up call. Mary McDonnell and Tahmoh Penikett star

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Galactica crew finally locates the legendary planet Kobol, where the ancient scrolls say humankind originated. However, as the ship descends to explore the fabled world, Cylon raiders appear and launch a strike - and they have plenty of resources waiting to join the battle

programme.title: The New Girlfriend (2014)
programme.desc: A woman grieving the death of her lifelong best friend gets to know her husband, only to discover he secretly likes wearing his late wife's clothes. She helps him to accept his transvestism, and teaches him to create a new female alter ego for himself. Drama based on a short story by Ruth Rendell, starring Anais Demoustier and Romain Duris. In French

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries focused on families, including his trilogy of documentaries centred around the Westboro Baptist Church. He examines how the death of patriarch Fred Phelps has left members of the church divided. He also reflects on how physical and mental health or forces beyond control can threaten to derail family lives

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood view footage of a hailstone the size of a rugby ball, a wall of ice that engulfed a town, and a frog that can freeze and unfreeze

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: This time it's an exclusive playlist of some of the very best songs from the series

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Young magician and Britain's Got Talent semi-finalist Aiden McCann, teaches the BP presenters how to pull off the perfect illusion. And see how Mwaksy gets on as she attempts to overcome her fear of dogs, with the ever-trusty Henry by her side to help out.

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and Vinny search for a pet

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Poppy becomes Amelia's personal trainer

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve searches for the greatest terrors from the skies, such as the bird-like Microraptor or the possibly largest flier ever - Hatzegopteryx

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The peregrine falcon is the fastest animal on earth, able to reach speeds of more than 200mph. But what does it take to be a high-speed record-breaker? This documentary follows a young family of falcons as they grow up in Chicago. It also meets renowned bird handler Lloyd Buck as he puts his peregrines to the test, using state-of-the-art technology. He also trains one young peregrine for the ultimate challenge of speed and agility, racing an Olympic skier in the Swiss Alps

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the first of two programmes in which they put their canine chums and feline friends under the microscope, with the aim of answering the age-old question - which are best, cats or dogs? The animals go head to head in a series of rounds testing different aspects, beginning in this edition with intelligence, sensory powers and physical prowess. On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris comes face to face with a pack of wolves, while feline fan Liz confronts an Arabian wild cat to discover how the relationship between humans and their pets have evolved

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie heads to a friend's farm where a young calf has developed a serious limp. The calf's protective mother is making it clear she wants her baby back, and Jennie does not want to distress her, so has to work fast. Vet Darren treats a two-day-old lamb, whose owner thinks has a urinary infection. However, when she happily relieves herself all over Darren's examination table, he has to use all his investigative skills to diagnose the real problem. Maurice King, the founder of the Downe Veterinary Clinic, encounters a loggerhead turtle has been rescued by the local sea life sanctuary

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnie's labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore more of the nation's dishes, beginning by showing there is more to British fowl than roast chicken. They prepare duck tikka and a one-pot partridge recipes before tasting a traditional Yorkshire Christmas pie and revealing the history of the Norfolk black turkey

programme.title: Romeo & Juliet (2013)
programme.desc: The children of two feuding noble houses fall in love, and marry in secret with the aid of a wily priest who hopes to reconcile the two families. However, when the conflict flares up again with fatal consequences, it sets in motion a chain of events that threatens to tear the lovers apart. Adaptation of Shakespeare's tragedy, starring Douglas Booth, Hailee Steinfeld and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Interviews with Hollywood actors whose time in the limelight began when they were still children, including Shirley Temple, Hayley Mills, Jack Wild, Macaulay Culkin, Christian Bale, Jodie Foster, and the young stars of the Harry Potter franchise. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on the strange world of animal communication, including scientists who are interpreting the howl of the wolf. Lucy Cooke discovers the communication skills required to persuade a penguin to swim and also heads to the Bahamas, where new research claims to have found that sharks could be social, make friendships and even have personalities. Conservationist Giles Clark is in Australia to see how technology is saving the koala and biologist Patrick Aryee is in South Africa to investigate the seductive calls of a lovestruck lioness

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad is joined by Alex Scott to present a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Culzean Castle on the west coast of Scotland in Ayrshire, experts Anita Manning and Natasha Raskin Sharp are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. A watercolour with a local connection catches Anita's eye, while Natasha is drawn to a gorgeous handwoven blanket. Paul Martin explores a hidden part of Culzean's history, uncovering the tales of smugglers working the coastline in the caves beneath the castle walls

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin looks back on the making of Pole to Pole, in which he travelled along a single line of longitude across the USSR, Africa and finally into Antarctica. Along the way, he paid a visit to Chernobyl and gained an insight into the last days of the Soviet Union. His travels across Africa led to an encounter with some fellow globetrotters from his home town of Sheffield - but he was forced to make a detour when his planned route passed through a war zone

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, James Crawford examines how aerial photography has revealed Scotland's hidden ancient past and uncovered secrets buried beneath the ground, including the discovery of hundreds of pizza ovens that once fed Rome's mighty legions, and the outline of a lost ornamental garden

programme.title: Royal Academy Summer Exhibition
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark and Brenda Emmanus present the best of this year's event, a show with challenges like no other in its 252-year history. Filmed both before and throughout lockdown, this documentary features artists such as this year's co-ordinators Jane and Louise Wilson, Brian Eno, Cornelia Parker and rising star Joy Labinjo. Along the way, the film charts the journeys of three members of the public who have submitted art for consideration in this year's show - which ones will see their work share space on the walls with the likes of Grayson Perry or Tracey Emin?

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: An aspiring writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Memories of the late '60s and early '70s, a period when inequality, poverty and racism fanned the flames of radical black politics and resulted in a harder soul sound. Stax Records went from being a label built around an integrated house band to becoming a black-centric business spearheaded by Isaac Hayes' expansive, flamboyant soul symphonies. Meanwhile, the Temptations tackled socially aware subjects like the Detroit riot and absent fathers, while Marvin Gaye conceived the epic What's Going On. Narrated by Carleen Anderson, with contributions from Mavis Staples, David Porter, Al Bell, Otis Williams, Barrett Strong and Fred Wesley

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night (1967)
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter K, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don prepares his pond for the season ahead and prunes a climbing rose. Adam Frost offers ideas for a winter display of containers and the programme catches up with Frances Tophill on her allotment in Kent, to find out about her growing year. Plus, another chance to see Carol Klein's autumnal visit to Hidcote Gardens in Gloucestershire, and a couple whose garden has been inspired by the Far East

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond joins chef Nick Nairn at one the most beautiful fishing locations in Scotland, the Lake of Menteith. The duo fish for rainbow trout then cook a delicious dish by the side of the lake. Anne Lundon investigates the need to adapt the landscape as extreme weather events become more frequent amid climate change. In Dundee, Arlene Stuart meets a furniture-maker who crafts pieces from brewing waste. Plus, entomologist Gabby Flynn reveals why wasps are vital for the ecosystem

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Chris Beardshaw and wife Frances take on a new allotment and are starting from scratch to create an easy, low-maintenance fruit and vegetable patch. Kirsty Wilson is looking to extend the season by using late-flowering perennials that complement autumn colours and Brian Cunningham takes advantage of the season to plant cost-effective bare root trees. To conclude the season, the team enjoys a video call - including a surprise visitor to the party

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: In the final episode, Mary travels to the Scottish Highlands. Surrounded by magical scenery, snow-capped mountains and frost covered forests, Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a sausage and pepper hotpot, simple twice-baked souffles, and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Asia in South Korea, a country whose food is set to be the next big thing in the UK. On a night out with young people in the capital Seoul, they sample the Korean take on barbecued food and fried chicken, then head south for a local squid festival

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman collects TV and radio presenter Nicholas Parsons in a Wolseley motor, and takes him back to Studland, Dorset, where he went on holiday in 1939. Len surprises Nicholas with an authentic 1930s campervan in the spot he originally camped in 76 years ago, before heading out on their bicycles. Later, they dine of a meal of mince and spuds, enjoy a game of cricket, and play Just a Minute after a quick dip in the sea

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Essex, the crew at Walton and Frinton gets an unusual mayday call to a ship called HMS Pickle. Arriving on scene, they discover it is not the modern naval vessel they expected, but a replica of a ship that once served in Nelson's fleet, and though the original survived the battle of Trafalgar, this one is shipping water and sinking fast. On the east coast of Scotland, the crew at Anstruther faces a tricky rescue trying to extract a walker with a badly broken leg from a remote coastal path. And in the Shannon basin in Ireland, the volunteers at one of the RNLI's few inland stations at Lough Derg are woken in the early hours of the morning by their pagers - cries for help have been heard coming from the 40-mile-long lake. After launching into inky darkness and thick fog, the crew make an eerie discovery

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs pitch more money-making ideas to panellists Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones. Two Syrian refugees share their alternative to Cypriot halloumi, a couple showcase some virus filtration gadgets, and a charitable entrepreneur has a plan for second-hand clothes. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at those who resisted slavery, including the famous Underground Railroad and freedom boats that helped runaway slaves to escape to the safety of Canada, using a network of safe houses to make the perilous journey north. The programme also examines the influence of famous freedom fighter Harriet Tubman and the black soldiers who served in the Union Army during the Civil War

programme.title: Detroit (2017)
programme.desc: A group of rogue police officers responds to a complaint with apparent retribution rather than justice in mind, launching a raid on a group of African-Americans. Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The extraordinary group face moments of peril and pain in New York's Kirby Plaza as Isaac's predictions for the future begin to unfold. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere and Masi Oka

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Four months after Peter and Sylar's showdown in New York City, Hiro remains in the past, while Claire and her family have started a new life in California. New heroes have emerged, including siblings Alejandro and Maya, who are on the run in the Dominican Republic, and Monica Dawson in New Orleans. Masi Oka and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter enters the glitzy world of awards and prizes - for books, movies, as well as poetry, museums and gardens - examining how they influence what art is created and what culture society consumes. She also reflects on if the pandemic has helped people see beyond the glamour and red carpets and reassess what prizes are really for. Contributors include Emmy-nominated actor Brian Cox, and Bernardine Evaristo, who last year shared the Booker Prize with Margaret Atwood

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Presenter Sara Cox is joined by comedians Phil Wang, Sara Pascoe and Tom Allen and singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor. The panellists share their favourite books, revealing those that make them laugh and the fictional characters they would like to meet. They also discuss this week's book group pick, The Midnight Library by Matt Haig and Sara Pascoe's latest book, Sex, Power, Money

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first broadcast on January 18, featuring performances by Halo James, the Quireboys, 49ers, New Kids on the Block, Megadeth, Kylie Minogue and Martika

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Jackie Brambles host the edition first aired on January 26, with performances by artists including Phil Collins, Public Enemy, Sinead O'Connor, Del Amitri, Kylie Minogue and Yell!

programme.title: Summer Holiday (1963)
programme.desc: Four mechanics embark on a fun-filled trip across Europe on board a double-decker London bus, and enjoy all manner of romantic adventures with three young women who hitch a ride on their way to Athens. However, an unexpected stowaway causes a few surprises. Musical comedy, starring Cliff Richard, Lauri Peters, Melvyn Hayes, Una Stubbs, Ron Moody and the Shadows

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard at the BBC
programme.desc: As the Peter Pan of Pop reaches his 80th birthday, a look back through its archives at some of his most memorable performances and biggest hits. Amongst the classic songs featured are Move It, The Young Ones, Living Doll, Bachelor Boy, the Eurovision Song Contest contenders Congratulations and Power to All Our Friends, Devil Woman, Miss You Nights, Wired for Sound and Cliff's much-praised '80s duet with Van Morrison, Wherever God Shines His Light

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Archive clips featuring solo artists, including music by performers as diverse as Sandie Shaw, Dusty Springfield, Long John Baldry, Lulu, Tom Jones, Brenton Wood, Cliff Richard, Cilla Black and Peter Sarstedt

programme.title: From Elton John to Gary Barlow: Celebrating 100 Concerts Live at Eden
programme.desc: A look back over the last 18 years of the Eden Sessions. Introduced by Nile Rodgers, who headlined the 100th show, highlights include Pulp, who headlined the first one in July 2002. Set against the backdrop of the Eden biomes, there are also performances by Duran Duran, Bastille, Muse, Gary Barlow, Van Morrison, Madness, Primal Scream and Lionel Richie

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard at the BBC
programme.desc: As the Peter Pan of Pop reaches his 80th birthday, a look back through its archives at some of his most memorable performances and biggest hits. Amongst the classic songs featured are Move It, The Young Ones, Living Doll, Bachelor Boy, the Eurovision Song Contest contenders Congratulations and Power to All Our Friends, Devil Woman, Miss You Nights, Wired for Sound and Cliff's much-praised '80s duet with Van Morrison, Wherever God Shines His Light

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto studies the city during the 18th century, and its fascination with art and sex. Casanova became an overnight celebrity and courtesans openly plied their trade, while great art was produced by Canaletto and Canova, and music by Vivaldi. But the hedonism could not go on indefinitely as first syphilis then the might of Napoleon's armies took their toll upon the city

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The globetrotting presenter arrives in Japan, where he meets a dedicated Monty Python fan, receives a beating in a Zen monastery, before visiting Nagasaki - where the second atom bomb was dropped in 1945. He then heads to the South Korean capital Seoul, where he attends a wedding ceremony, before briefly crossing the border into North Korea at the Joint Security Area in Panmunjeom

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: A Swedish film unit arrives to shoot a period drama in Vigata, and Augello immediately takes a shine to the production's female lead. When Montalbano intervenes in a case of bullying at a local school, a resident asks the inspector to unearth the story behind a collection of mysterious home movies found in his dead father's attic. Luca Zingaretti stars. In Italian

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The Malmo police receive a ransom video from Hans' kidnapper, and Saga is placed under further strain when her mother turns up at the station unexpectedly. The pair's family spat is quickly interrupted when Henrik announces that victim Helle Anker's car has been located, and the subsequent chain of evidence leads to a gruesome discovery at an amusement park. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Rikard Jonsson escapes after violently assaulting another victim, and Saga believes Lise Friis Anderson might be his next target. However, when police visit her house, they discover Rikard has taken a hostage. Meanwhile, Jeanette picks up a bag at Malmo railway station, as requested, but quickly finds herself at the mercy of two thugs who take her cargo for themselves. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: The production of fruit and vegetables in Britain. programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The explorer finds that China is a world of contrasts and contradictions as he treks toward the Vietnam border via Shanghai and the Yangtze river. From the Bavarian-style hotel at Qingdao, once occupied by Chairman Mao, he tackles the 6,293 steps of China's holiest mountain, sees the world's largest engineering project and visits the ancient Miao tribe in the mountains of the south-west

programme.title: Collateral
programme.desc: A pizza delivery man is shot dead in south London, seemingly in a random act of violence, but DI Kip Glaspie is unconvinced and discovers that the takeaway manager inexplicably sent a Syrian refugee instead of his regular driver. Kip and her partner DS Nathan Bilk track down the victim's home, where they find his sisters, who the detective is convinced are hiding something. Crime thriller by David Hare, starring Carey Mulligan, John Simm, Nicola Walker and Billie Piper

programme.title: Collateral
programme.desc: Pizza shop manager Laurie has gone missing and a fatigued Kip discovers a hidden stash of cash while searching his house for clues, while Nathan learns Mikey is using Regal Pizza as a front to deliver drugs. programme.desc: A guided tour by 16-year-old Syrian boy, Ahmed, through one of the biggest refugee camps in the world - Zaatari in Jordan. Ahmed is like any other boy his age, except he's had to leave everything behind, including his toys and technology, to move to the refugee camp. Life in Zaatari is nothing compared to home. When the Syrian civil war began in 2012 his family had to abandon their affluent lives and flee across the border to Zaatari camp with thousands of others for safety. Now living in a storage container for a home after his house was destroyed in the civil war, he has lived in Zaatari for three years, along with 80,000 other refugees, who can't leave the camp without permission. Despite their camp having everything they need, from shops to a hospital, school and football pitches, life is very different to how it was before. Ahmed doesn't let this affect his positivity though, and he finds new ways to have fun with his friends - even if it is in a desert sandstorm followed by flood

programme.desc: Catching up with three Syrian child refugees

Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Strictly Amy: Crohn's and Me

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Leeds Rhinos v Salford Red Devils (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the final from Wembley Stadium. With analysis from Jon Wilkin, Jamie Peacock and Shaun Wane, and commentary by Dave Woods

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes on another walk around Wales, this time travelling from Porth Swtan to Cemlyn Bay, where he visits a house restored by locals and tries his hand at whittling

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Another four famous faces try the famous black chair out for size. They are antiques expert Philip Serrell, poet and presenter Lemn Sissay, Sky Sports anchor Olivia Wayne, and comedian Richard McCourt - better known as one half of the comedy duo Dick and Dom. John Humphrys asks the questions

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous sitcom actors take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tim Healy, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Kerry Godliman, Tom Basden, Mina Anwar, Serena Evans, Judy Cornwell and Jeremy Gittins

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the launch of this year's Strictly Come Dancing, joined in the ballroom by 12 brand new celebrity contestants, and judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood and Shirley Ballas. As well as revealing this year's pairings, the show will feature two spectacular group routines from the Strictly professionals, a very special music performance from Joel Corry and MNEK, and the return of reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Lynne and Graham, a couple from Merthyr Tydfi in Wales, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park and Manchester City v Arsenal at Etihad Stadium. It was 10 years to the day that Everton last recorded a victory over their neighbours, but they had started the season well. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking to avenge Arsenal's 2-0 victory when the sides last met three months ago in an FA Cup semi-final. Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the game between Kansas City Chiefs and Buffalo Bills and look ahead to the other fixtures in week six

programme.title: Dream House (2011)
programme.desc: A man moves to a small town with his wife and two daughters, but receives the startling news that a woman and her children were murdered in his new home five years previously. He begins seeing things which make him suspect the house is haunted, and that there may be a connection between the ghosts and his own past. Horror, starring Daniel Craig, Naomi Watts and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sima Kotecha present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
