Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team visit a flat in Liverpool, an old church in Denholme, West Yorkshire, and a ground-floor flat in East Croydon, London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At Epsom Racecourse Antiques and Collectables Fair in Surrey, experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper help to guide the teams to winning a golden gavel. Presented by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Cheddar Gorge in Somerset to hear the origins of the hymn Rock of Ages, while Gemma Hunt meets worship leader Martin Smith and new Christian group Bright City

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in the Cornish countryside house-hunting with a couple with a £350,000 budget who are planning an escape from the busy suburbs of Surrey

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Wales, upcycling expert EJ Osborne uses his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre near Abergavenny by searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A pair of bench ends are worked on by woodworking specialists Josh and Oli, while designer maker Rupert Blanchard is given the tall order of transforming a tallboy chest of drawers. For his own project, EJ has a collection of glass jars to keep him busy

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following the Cory and Tredegar brass bands as they prepare to battle it out at the Welsh Regional Championships in Wrexham. As well as looking at the history of brass bands, the film hears from members including Tim, who overcame his tearaway youth to excel at the tuba, and Rob, who postponed his kidney transplant to play in the competition

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Bespoke dress designer Samantha Buca returns to help more brides find the perfect wedding dress. In this first edition, she meets Lynsey, who wants a Game of Thrones-inspired dress for her vow renewal. Meanwhile, Ruby would like a Bollywood-style party frock, and Natasha is hoping to find a dream gown for her wedding to Sarah

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The social experiment continues as the Griffins, Cartwrights and Phillips struggle with life as it was in a 1920s mining community. Exams are just around the corner for the children and the men live in the reality of the coalfield, where pit closures are normal and poor wages all too common

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent events, from a possible Banksy to an African spear linked to the explorer David Livingstone. Other finds include a Dior necklace, a collection of 1960s airline cabin bags and a remarkable skeleton clock, as well as two items related to the Suffragettes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Celebrating his win in a newspaper libel case, cabinet minister Peter Laurence is summoned to Downing Street to see PM Dawn Ellison, who reveals she is looking to promote him to an office of state. However, he is soon bought back down to earth with a bump when his special adviser Duncan Knock reveals an inmate in a women's prison is claiming to have a secret about his past that could affect his future. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Crystal Palace v Brighton & Hove Albion at Selhurst Park and Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Palace and Brighton shared the spoils in a 1-1 draw here last season, and Spurs won 2-0 against their London rivals here four months ago. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester Untied

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob catches up with celebrated Turner Prize-winning artist Chris Ofili, as he completes work on The Caged Bird's Song, a new piece set to receive its exhibition debut at London's National Gallery. Best known as a painter, Ofili switches mediums with his latest piece, working alongside expert weavers in Edinburgh to create a wool tapestry seven meters wide and three meters tall. With The Caged Bird's Song, Ofili aims to demonstrate his ongoing fascination with classical mythology and contemporary 'demigods', while also paying tribute to the landscape of his adopted island home of Trinidad

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets the residents of town in Kent that's slowly sliding into the sea, to see how they are coping with their perilous situation. Plus, the automatic bill switching services that have left some customers wondering whether they'd have been better off doing it themselves

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three flats in a terrible state of repair in a corner property in Wolverhampton and two terraces in Mountain Ash in Wales and Chatham in Kent become available on the market. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell take a look and follow progress as one seasoned developer and two novices tackle their projects

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem and Kamini uncover the real plan behind New Trefelin, and it begins to look as if foul play may be the cause of the mine's collapse. If it's no accident, Aled and Daf are poised to become the prime suspects - but they protest their innocence. Meanwhile, Robert pleads with Megan to leave the village with him, but Prem confronts him with the truth about his past and Basil's real intentions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: In the final instalment of their culinary journey through the history of British cuisine, Si King and Dave Myers explore comfort food, discussing how certain ingredients and recipes can give people a sense of well-being, such as chicken noodle soup and a simple fish dish

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the Mackintosh at the Willow tearooms in Glasgow to celebrate all things 20th century and collectable. Plus, expert Ben Cooper gives the lowdown on his family connection with Whitefriars glass and Tim Weeks shows off some collectable tech

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Carl Hutchinson present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond joins chef Nick Nairn at one the most beautiful fishing locations in Scotland, the Lake of Menteith. The duo fish for rainbow trout then cook a delicious dish by the side of the lake. Anne Lundon investigates the need to adapt the landscape as extreme weather events become more frequent amid climate change. In Dundee, Arlene Stuart meets a furniture-maker who crafts pieces from brewing waste. Plus, entomologist Gabby Flynn reveals why wasps are vital for the ecosystem

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is furious with Lola for telling Denise he has been seeing Raymond. Peter gives Kathy's dating app a little tweak in the hope she will have more luck. Sharon worries that Bobby's fears may be getting out of control, while Kush is forced to pay up in exchange for his silence

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts the satirical quiz, inviting journalist Helen Lewis and comedian, actress and writer Janey Godley to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Legendary football manager Arsene Wenger talks about his autobiography My Life in Red and White, while Hollywood star Samuel L Jackson - joined by his wife LaTanya Richardson Jackson - discusses presenting the documentary Enslaved: The Lost History of the Transatlantic Slave Trade. Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora impulsively dyes her hair after a fight with her father, and Wally goes to a single-parent support group meeting. Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Nora suffers a vaginal injury, she's tapped by a DJ to record her chronic queefing for his new track. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. The team is joined by social media's Robert Borthwick, a man known for his wit and whimsy on Twitter that riles and rallies fans of every club in equal measure

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the Mackintosh at the Willow tearooms in Glasgow to celebrate all things 20th century and collectable. Plus, expert Ben Cooper gives the lowdown on his family connection with Whitefriars glass and Tim Weeks shows off some collectable tech

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem and Kamini uncover the real plan behind New Trefelin, and it begins to look as if foul play may be the cause of the mine's collapse. If it's no accident, Aled and Daf are poised to become the prime suspects - but they protest their innocence. Meanwhile, Robert pleads with Megan to leave the village with him, but Prem confronts him with the truth about his past and Basil's real intentions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: In the final instalment of their culinary journey through the history of British cuisine, Si King and Dave Myers explore comfort food, discussing how certain ingredients and recipes can give people a sense of well-being, such as chicken noodle soup and a simple fish dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Carl Hutchinson present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they can't resist getting involved with Thomas's tale as well

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is furious with Lola for telling Denise he has been seeing Raymond. Peter gives Kathy's dating app a little tweak in the hope she will have more luck. Sharon worries that Bobby's fears may be getting out of control, while Kush is forced to pay up in exchange for his silence

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts the satirical quiz, inviting journalist Helen Lewis and comedian, actress and writer Janey Godley to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Legendary football manager Arsene Wenger talks about his autobiography My Life in Red and White, while Hollywood star Samuel L Jackson - joined by his wife LaTanya Richardson Jackson - discusses presenting the documentary Enslaved: The Lost History of the Transatlantic Slave Trade. Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora impulsively dyes her hair after a fight with her father, and Wally goes to a single-parent support group meeting. Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Nora suffers a vaginal injury, she's tapped by a DJ to record her chronic queefing for his new track. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Donna Hay, and special guest Sara Cox. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: This time, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for low cost baking, using ingredients that do not break the bank or compromise on flavour. Dishes include fluffy onion pretzels, a potato rosti quiche, rhubarb and custard kisses, and her signature rich and indulgent chocolate fondant

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton and Manchester City v Arsenal

programme.title: Rob Burrow: My Year with MND
programme.desc: Rugby league star Rob Burrow talks about his fight against motor neurone disease, and how his rugby career has prepared him for the challenges he faces

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Leeds Rhinos v Salford Red Devils (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the final from Wembley Stadium. Rhinos were in good form as they defeated Wigan Warriors 26-12 in the semi-finals, while Salford produced a fine comeback to prevail 24-22 against holders Warrington Wolves. With analysis from Jon Wilkin, Jamie Peacock and Shaun Wane, and commentary by Dave Woods

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Another four famous faces try the famous black chair out for size. They are antiques expert Philip Serrell, poet and presenter Lemn Sissay, Sky Sports anchor Olivia Wayne, and comedian Richard McCourt - better known as one half of the comedy duo Dick and Dom. John Humphrys asks the questions

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous sitcom actors take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tim Healy, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Kerry Godliman, Tom Basden, Mina Anwar, Serena Evans, Judy Cornwell and Jeremy Gittins

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the launch of this year's Strictly Come Dancing, joined in the ballroom by 12 brand new celebrity contestants, and judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood and Shirley Ballas. As well as revealing this year's pairings, the show will feature two spectacular group routines from the Strictly professionals, a very special music performance from Joel Corry and MNEK, and the return of reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Lynne and Graham, a couple from Merthyr Tydfi in Wales, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park and Manchester City v Arsenal at Etihad Stadium. It was 10 years to the day that Everton last recorded a victory over their neighbours, but they had started the season well. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking to avenge Arsenal's 2-0 victory when the sides last met three months ago in an FA Cup semi-final. Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the game between Kansas City Chiefs and Buffalo Bills and look ahead to the other fixtures in week six

programme.title: Dream House (2011)
programme.desc: A man moves to a small town with his wife and two daughters, but receives the startling news that a woman and her children were murdered in his new home five years previously. He begins seeing things which make him suspect the house is haunted, and that there may be a connection between the ghosts and his own past. Horror, starring Daniel Craig, Naomi Watts and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sima Kotecha present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park and Manchester City v Arsenal at Etihad Stadium. It was 10 years to the day that Everton last recorded a victory over their neighbours, but they had started the season well. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking to avenge Arsenal's 2-0 victory when the sides last met three months ago in an FA Cup semi-final. Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and pays a visit to a local jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Cheddar Gorge in Somerset to hear the origins of the hymn Rock of Ages, while Gemma Hunt meets worship leader Martin Smith and new Christian group Bright City

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple returning from living in Sweden for 25 years who want to find a home in Wiltshire, and goes for a ride with a professional carriage driver

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Wales, upcycling expert EJ Osborne uses his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre near Abergavenny by searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A pair of bench ends are worked on by woodworking specialists Josh and Oli, while designer maker Rupert Blanchard is given the tall order of transforming a tallboy chest of drawers. For his own project, EJ has a collection of glass jars to keep him busy

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent events, from a possible Banksy to an African spear linked to the explorer David Livingstone. Other finds include a Dior necklace, a collection of 1960s airline cabin bags and a remarkable skeleton clock, as well as two items related to the Suffragettes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Celebrating his win in a newspaper libel case, cabinet minister Peter Laurence is summoned to Downing Street to see PM Dawn Ellison, who reveals she is looking to promote him to an office of state. However, he is soon bought back down to earth with a bump when his special adviser Duncan Knock reveals an inmate in a women's prison is claiming to have a secret about his past that could affect his future. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Crystal Palace v Brighton & Hove Albion at Selhurst Park and Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Palace and Brighton shared the spoils in a 1-1 draw here last season, and Spurs won 2-0 against their London rivals here four months ago. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester Untied

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob catches up with celebrated Turner Prize-winning artist Chris Ofili, as he completes work on The Caged Bird's Song, a new piece set to receive its exhibition debut at London's National Gallery. Best known as a painter, Ofili switches mediums with his latest piece, working alongside expert weavers in Edinburgh to create a wool tapestry seven meters wide and three meters tall. With The Caged Bird's Song, Ofili aims to demonstrate his ongoing fascination with classical mythology and contemporary 'demigods', while also paying tribute to the landscape of his adopted island home of Trinidad

programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie sets a plan against Belle in motion, Leyla and Gabby plan something big and ambitious, and Dan's determined to start providing for his family again

programme.title: Coronation Street

Evelyn and Arthur plan a camping trip to Norfolk and Billy reveals to Paul that the bishop has recommended he apply for the position of archdeacon

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a seemingly beloved resident of a coastal community is fatally driven off the road, and realises she has to dig up the victim's past to have any chance of unmasking the killer. Joined by her faithful team, including DC Kenny Lockhart and new recruit DC Jac Williams, Vera attempts to find the missing link that will crack the case, but finds it difficult to get to the truth when everyone they encounter seems to be protecting a secret. Crime drama based on Ann Cleeves' series of novels, starring Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: John Bishop's Great Whale Rescue
programme.desc: Part two of two. John's mission to return Little White and Little Grey from captivity to the Icelandic beluga whale sanctuary continues, as the pair begin the 6,000-mile journey to reach their new home. Following the previous delays in China, the release is already two months behind schedule, and as autumn approaches the whales have little time to put on enough weight and acclimatise to the cold water they will face in the bay

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Despite heavy casualties, the British are instructed to continue fighting at all costs. Walter's grip on reality weakens, and Matthew becomes his unlikely saviour. Aware that Vera is also in imminent danger, he rushes to save her and escape from Singapore, but, just as they are reunited, fresh trouble awaits. David Morrissey stars

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: The staff prepare to let their hair down at their annual gala, where they celebrate their most profitable year and busiest season. programme.title: Alvin and the Chipmunks: Chipwrecked (2011)
programme.desc: The singing rodents cause mayhem while performing on a tropical cruise, before they find themselves stranded on a desert island. As they search for a way to get back to civilisation, it becomes clear that they are not alone. Comedy sequel, starring Jason Lee and Jenny Slate, with the voices of Justin Long, Matthew Gray Gubler and Jesse McCartney

programme.title: The Boxtrolls (2014)
programme.desc: The people of a small town live in fear of underground trolls, who they do not realise are actually harmless. A boy raised by the creatures must convince the townsfolk there is nothing to fear when a team of pest exterminators is hired to wipe out the friendly monsters. Stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Isaac Hempstead Wright, Elle Fanning and Ben Kingsley

programme.title: Shark Tale (2004)
programme.desc: Ambitious fish Oscar dreams of escaping his humdrum undersea life. His wish comes true when a gangster shark keen to escape a world of organised crime seeks his help in faking his own death. But Oscar soon finds himself out of his depth as his fearsome reputation gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Smith, Robert De Niro, Martin Scorsese, Renee Zellweger, Angelina Jolie and Jack Black

programme.desc: The Griffins provide DVD commentary on a recent episode and reveal never-before-told drama between Peter and Lois

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian compete for the inheritance of a wealthy, older heiress. Peter and Chris battle for Mr Herbert's affection

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Brian go head to head to be crowned the new face of Pawtucket Ale when the brewery's bosses decide to change the company mascot

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A disturbance in Stan's daily bedtime routine unleashes his alter-ego, a do-gooder intent on completing all the heroic deeds he would never normally even contemplate. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger forms an elaborate plan involving swapping identities to help Steve get a date with a girl at his school, while Stan and Francine become air stewardesses to thwart an evil plot to blow up the sun by guest star Mark Cuban

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Roberta becomes obsessed with her dreams of being a superstar, and Cleveland enters Stoolbend's annual boat race, but gets distracted when his celebrity idol moves to town. He is then forced to make a difficult decision when his new friend asks him to join his bowling team

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland challenges Donna's ex-husband Robert to a Coolympics competition, hoping to prove his hip credentials to Cleveland Jr. Featuring the guest voice of Snoop Dogg

programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as Russell Kane, Jorgie Porter, Paige Turley, Suzi Ruffell, Radzi Chinyanganya and David Potts try to win some normal people £2,500 and a pet-food dispenser

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert teaches Angie to drive but it proves disastrous

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The folk of Aidensfield panic when rumours of an escaped killer are confirmed. Rob attempts to track down the fugitive, but his investigations are complicated when Helen gets caught up in the case. David is left reeling when Greengrass's lurcher Alfred becomes seriously ill, and Peggy speaks to bereavement expert Bernie in the hope that he can lend support to the devastated handyman. Rural drama with David Stockwell

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris winds Kim up about paying him the money she owes. Fiona is determined to make Tony suffer. Becky figures out Fiona's riddle and she and Tony rush to find Will

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Becky and Tony are shocked to find Fiona and Will holding hands. Rachel hands in her notice at the farm. Will loses all respect for Tony when he hears the truth about Guatemala

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen regrets her night of passion with Curly. Don refuses to be pampered by the Platts. Liz worries over Steve and fears Fraser might be using him to get to her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera gets emotional at the thought of Curly leaving. Don refuses to talk to a doctor. Emily lures Curly to the Rovers where all the residents throw a surprise party for him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin search for a nursery now that Janice has left, and Mrs Tishell is shocked to find out that Al has very high blood pressure. Louisa also helps with the diagnosis of a pupil, which spurs her on to explore the possibilities of a change of career. This is not welcome news for Martin, who dislikes any kind of change, even if it could be for the better. Elsewhere, Ruth is approached about selling her farm. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz, Selina Cadell and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP is perturbed when he discovers Louisa, who is already spending much of her time away from home, has applied to take up a child and adolescent counselling course. However, achieving a work-life balance is far from the greatest of concerns for some of the Ellinghams' neighbours, not least of whom is Al, whose plans to demonstrate his business acumen are placed in jeopardy when Ken decides to close the pub. Unless Ruth agrees to serve as a guarantor, Ken will not allow Morwenna and Al to take over the establishment, so the pair arrange a race-walking event to raise funds and prove the community is on their side

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie turns her attention to Brentford house and the theory that one or more of the suspects may have been abused at the gatherings there. Marion is questioned again after Sinead's allegation that David could have been an IRA target. The detectives also look further into the accusations made against Colin while he was working in the city - but something does not quite seem to add up. Was his alleged victim telling the truth - and if not, why would she lie? Cold-case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: With the suspects in the murder of David Walker all having either a motive, opportunity or connection to the victim, Cassie and Sunny are left with a massive puzzle to solve. Cassie soon focuses in on Sara, whose alibi seems too perfect, as if it has been put together by an accomplice of the ex-prostitute - and the team is soon investigating the possibility of a wider revenge plan. Meanwhile, interviews with Colin and Marion's loved ones prove revealing. Cold case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Badria Timimi, Mark Bonnar and Rosie Cavaliero

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Carry On Cleo (1964)
programme.desc: A captured Briton is mistaken for a heroic swordsman and promoted to the Roman Royal Guard, with orders to protect Julius Caesar. But the last thing he and the fated emperor expect is an assassination attempt from within Caesar's own circle of friends. Comedy, starring Amanda Barrie, Kenneth Williams, Sid James, Kenneth Connor, Jim Dale, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bell-ringers of Midsomer Wellow are mysteriously murdered one by one - and it seems an ancient rhyme may hold the key to the killer's motive. As Barnaby and Troy embark on a baffling investigation in an attempt to determine the truth, Joyce starts digging into the past to discover the assassin's motives. John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Hugh Bonneville star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A pupil at the prestigious Devington School dies of a stab wound while competing in a race in honour of his recently deceased grandfather. DCI Barnaby discovers the death could be linked to a long-running rivalry between the school and the locals - or to the youngster's membership of an elite club dedicated to dining on suet puddings. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Chief Superintendent Jack Cornish goes missing and a recently embalmed body is discovered near an isolated farm track - could there be a link? As Hathaway goes on sabbatical in Kosovo, Lewis takes an awkward young constable under his wing to investigate the case. Guest starring Lucy Speed and Peter Davison, with Kevin Whately and Babou Ceesay

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Carry On Doctor (1967)
programme.desc: A popular doctor is sacked after a misunderstanding ruins his reputation, prompting the furious patients to stage a revolt against the luckless medic's strait-laced superiors. Meanwhile, the hospital head has his own problems after becoming the unlikely romantic target of a strict matron. Comedy, with Frankie Howerd, Jim Dale, Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy investigate the death of amateur artist Ruth Fairfax, whose body has been discovered by Joyce - but the more they delve into the dead woman's background, the more mysterious she becomes. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, with guest appearances by John Sessions and Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: The Queen's Green Planet
programme.desc: Documentary following Her Majesty the Queen and an ambitious legacy project to create a global network of protected forests, spanning the 53 countries of the Commonwealth. The film also features a conversation between the Queen and Sir David Attenborough in the gardens of Buckingham Palace last summer, with the subjects ranging from climate change to conkers and birthday gifts. There is also footage of the members of the royal family involved so far in making the Queen's Commonwealth Canopy a reality, including Prince Harry planting trees in the Caribbean and Prince William in Canada's Great Bear Rainforest

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: When Kate's flashy ex-husband Dennis arrives in town with his much younger wife, Kate feigns an attachment to Koji. Councillor Bone is sure their relationship is a front, but that does not stop a good old-fashioned show down at the pub. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola and Barbara Flynn

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Not for the first time, Kate is being sued, but this time by Councillor Bone. Koji discovers he is under investigation for working illegally, and things do not improve when he tells Kate what he really thinks about her grandson. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's investigation into the death of a nameless Russian leads to a mysterious military facility run by Colonel Harry Galt, a respected soldier with an impeccable war record. However, as Foyle makes inquiries, he realises the deceased was a spy with dangerous connections to British Intelligence. Meanwhile, Sam does her best to help husband Adam during his by-election campaign, but can't help putting her foot in it. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Jonathan Hyde and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bell-ringers of Midsomer Wellow are mysteriously murdered one by one - and it seems an ancient rhyme may hold the key to the killer's motive. As Barnaby and Troy embark on a baffling investigation in an attempt to determine the truth, Joyce starts digging into the past to discover the assassin's motives. John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Hugh Bonneville star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A pupil at the prestigious Devington School dies of a stab wound while competing in a race in honour of his recently deceased grandfather. DCI Barnaby discovers the death could be linked to a long-running rivalry between the school and the locals - or to the youngster's membership of an elite club dedicated to dining on suet puddings. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Chief Superintendent Jack Cornish goes missing and a recently embalmed body is discovered near an isolated farm track - could there be a link? As Hathaway goes on sabbatical in Kosovo, Lewis takes an awkward young constable under his wing to investigate the case. Guest starring Lucy Speed and Peter Davison, with Kevin Whately and Babou Ceesay

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Carry On Doctor (1967)
programme.desc: A popular doctor is sacked after a misunderstanding ruins his reputation, prompting the furious patients to stage a revolt against the luckless medic's strait-laced superiors. Meanwhile, the hospital head has his own problems after becoming the unlikely romantic target of a strict matron. Comedy, with Frankie Howerd, Jim Dale, Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy investigate the death of amateur artist Ruth Fairfax, whose body has been discovered by Joyce - but the more they delve into the dead woman's background, the more mysterious she becomes. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, with guest appearances by John Sessions and Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: The Queen's Green Planet
programme.desc: Documentary following Her Majesty the Queen and an ambitious legacy project to create a global network of protected forests, spanning the 53 countries of the Commonwealth. The film also features a conversation between the Queen and Sir David Attenborough in the gardens of Buckingham Palace last summer, with the subjects ranging from climate change to conkers and birthday gifts. There is also footage of the members of the royal family involved so far in making the Queen's Commonwealth Canopy a reality, including Prince Harry planting trees in the Caribbean and Prince William in Canada's Great Bear Rainforest

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: When Kate's flashy ex-husband Dennis arrives in town with his much younger wife, Kate feigns an attachment to Koji. Councillor Bone is sure their relationship is a front, but that does not stop a good old-fashioned show down at the pub. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola and Barbara Flynn

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Not for the first time, Kate is being sued, but this time by Councillor Bone. Koji discovers he is under investigation for working illegally, and things do not improve when he tells Kate what he really thinks about her grandson. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's investigation into the death of a nameless Russian leads to a mysterious military facility run by Colonel Harry Galt, a respected soldier with an impeccable war record. However, as Foyle makes inquiries, he realises the deceased was a spy with dangerous connections to British Intelligence. Meanwhile, Sam does her best to help husband Adam during his by-election campaign, but can't help putting her foot in it. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Jonathan Hyde and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The team is hired to smuggle a Russian scientist into Britain who wishes to marry her childhood sweetheart. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar joins forces with a glamorous female agent in order to help liberate John Spring, a double agent whom the Russians believe to be working for them. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman hires Holmes and Watson to investigate the strange death of her sister, whose enigmatic final words seemingly hold the key to the case. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and King pose as a married couple hoping to enrol their son into the Alpha Academy for gifted youngsters. In reality however, the Academy appears to be a training facility with extra-terrestrial intentions. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur sells a less-than-roadworthy Austin Allegro to a nun - and it isn't long before a run of bad luck convinces him he's incurred the wrath of God. George Cole and Gary Webster play the entrepreneurial Daleys

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan becomes romantically involved with a crime journalist. However, when she is implicated in a major crime, her innocence and his professional loyalties are called into question. Carter also wavers, beginning to fear his boss's personal life has shadowed his judgement. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A woman is tormented by calls from her supposedly deceased husband, whose lifeless body was pulled from the sea several weeks earlier. While she is admitted to a sanatorium to prevent a nervous breakdown, Templar works on the premise that her spouse may indeed still be alive. Roger Moore and Sylvia Syms star

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A valuable gem is stolen from the Countess of Morcar, leading the famous detective into a bizarre investigation involving a recovered Christmas goose. Mystery, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: With King on holiday, Steed and aristocratic adventurer Lady Diana investigate a sinister factory housing a deadly computer that seems to be behind the murders of several British agents. Patrick Macnee and Jennifer Croxton star

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley champion cars from 1955. Vicki chooses the Mercedes 190SL, while Alex plumps for the British MGA sports car

programme.title: Goodwood Speedweek Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the motoring event from West Sussex, which brings together elements of the Festival of Speed and the Revival following the cancellation of those events. Plus, a selection of races only usually seen at the Members' Meetings

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley choose cars from 1980. Vicki likes the Audi Quattro, while Alex goes for a Renault 5 Turbo

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Members of a team of five train control centre workers get their chance to take on the Tower in a bid to make off with a big cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Coronation Street actress Samia Ghadie (Maria Connor), The Last Leg's Alex Brooker, presenter Annabel Giles and comedian Julian Clary compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Licence to Kill (1989)
programme.desc: While on leave to attend the wedding of his friend, CIA agent Felix Leiter, James Bond is asked to help with a secret mission to capture notorious drug baron, Franz Sanchez. Though successful, Sanchez later escapes and seeks revenge on Leiter, killing his new bride and leaving Leiter for dead. Incensed by this, Bond refuses his next mission from MI6 to go in search of Sanchez, leaving M with no alternative but to revoke Bond's licence to kill. Now considered a rogue agent, Bond goes undercover to pose as an assassin for hire within Sanchez's drug empire, to bring him down from the inside. Spy thriller, starring Timothy Dalton, Robert Davi, Carey Lowell, Talisa Soto and Benicio Del Toro

programme.desc: Doyle considers his future in CI5 after it transpires the father of his fiancee is under surveillance by the unit, suspected of being part of an international drug-smuggling ring. Patricia Hodge guest stars, with Martin Shaw

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Recently released criminal Eddie Boyse becomes prime suspect in an assault case - and Regan believes he is guilty. Unfortunately for the officer, Boyse has the perfect alibi - he was in Regan's company when the crime was committed. Drama, starring John Thaw, with Dennis Waterman and Del Henney

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur, Ray and Dave go on a day-trip to Calais, intending to stock the Winchester's cellars with duty-free plonk, but soon find themselves in trouble. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are at Ascot ahead of Champions Day 2020, where £2.5m prize money is up for grabs on a spectacular day of racing

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: A look back at football highlights from November 1974, hosted by former ITV presenter Brian Moore, featuring a full-blooded London derby in the old third division between Crystal Palace - managed by Malcolm Allison - and Charlton Athletic. Plus, action from Newcastle United against Manchester City and Luton Town against Burnley

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces more clips and profiles from the ITV sport archives, including a look at boxing star Nicola Adams, the story of rugby resilience in the Japanese city of Kamaishi, and golfer Seve Ballesteros playing for the cameras in the company of Jimmy Greaves

programme.title: World Cup Rivalries
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the most riveting matches played by the rival footballing nations

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights of the seventh meeting of the season from Croft in North Yorkshire, featuring races 19, 20 and 21 of the campaign. Last time out at Silverstone, a thrilling day of racing saw the gap between championship leader Ash Sutton and reigning champion Colin Turkington cut to just four points meaning there is everything to race for as the end of the season approaches. Presented by Steve Rider and Louise Goodman, commentary from David Addison, Tim Harvey, Richard John Neil and Paul O'Neill

programme.title: Rooster Cogburn (1975)
programme.desc: The former marshal returns to duty to track down outlaws who have stolen a shipment of explosives and gone on the rampage in a quiet town. However, the stubborn daughter of a preacher killed by the bandits insists on joining him so she can avenge her father's death. Western, starring John Wayne reprising his role from the original 1969 version of True Grit, with Katharine Hepburn and Anthony Zerbe

programme.title: DTM Highlights 2020
programme.desc: The final third of the 2020 DTM season takes the series back to Belgium, this time to Circuit Zolder in Round 7. The venue, built in 1963, actually hosted the very first race in DTM history, and was brought back into the calendar in 2019. Described as an old-school drivers' circuit, the layout of Zolder guarantees exciting races with little margin for error. Eventual champion Rene Rast and Philipp Eng were winners here last year

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The Daleys move into the high-spending world of corporate hospitality and find themselves caught between glamour and danger. Samantha Womack guest stars, alongside George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan adopts a new identity as an ex-con and goes undercover as a truck driver to expose a gang of ruthless hijackers. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee

programme.desc: While on leave to attend the wedding of his friend, CIA agent Felix Leiter, James Bond is asked to help with a secret mission to capture notorious drug baron, Franz Sanchez. Though successful, Sanchez later escapes and seeks revenge on Leiter, killing his new bride and leaving Leiter for dead. Incensed by this, Bond refuses his next mission from MI6 to go in search of Sanchez, leaving M with no alternative but to revoke Bond's licence to kill. Now considered a rogue agent, Bond goes undercover to pose as an assassin for hire within Sanchez's drug empire, to bring him down from the inside. Spy thriller, starring Timothy Dalton, Robert Davi, Carey Lowell, Talisa Soto and Benicio Del Toro

programme.desc: The classic rock band's career highlights, from Freddie Bulsara's early days in Zanzibar, to his life-changing days as Freddie Mercury with the band Smile and eventually Queen. programme.title: Dando: 20 Year Hunt for a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary re-examining the case of Jill Dando's murder, the BBC presenter who was mercilessly shot dead as she entered her Fulham home in April 1999. Two years after her death, Barry George was found guilty of her murder, but following a dramatic retrial in 2008, he was acquitted and released from prison. Now more than 20 years on, this programme looks back at some of the unanswered questions surrounding the case
